data.title: As a practitioner, sprint's manual-pause state persists across session interruptions with --resume recovery
data.description:
## Description

**What**: When sprint enters the manual-pause handshake, persist pause state to /tmp/sprint-pause-state-<epic-hash>.json (story list, verification_commands, audit-token requirements, in-progress handshake marker). On Ctrl-C / SIGURG / session kill, leave ticket state in a recoverable state (no orphan status transitions). On sprint re-invocation with an existing pause state file, offer --resume to continue the handshake where it left off.

**Why**: Completes SC5's resilience dimension — without persistence, a killed or interrupted sprint leaves manual stories half-processed with no recovery path; the user cannot distinguish "skipped intentionally" from "session died mid-handshake."

**Scope**:
- IN: Pause-state file write at handshake entry; state file schema mirroring the merge-to-main --resume pattern (JSON with TTL); signal-safe cleanup path that avoids orphan in_progress transitions; sprint re-invocation detects existing state file, prompts for --resume, and rehydrates the handshake; TTL-based stale-state cleanup. Flag-gated by planning.external_dependency_block_enabled.
- OUT: General sprint state persistence outside the pause handshake (merge-to-main covers merge; autonomous-phase state is already handled elsewhere); brainstorm/preplanning/implementation-plan persistence.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, entering the manual-pause handshake writes state to /tmp/sprint-pause-state-<epic-hash>.json including story list, verification commands, audit-token requirements, and an in-progress marker
  Satisfies: "sprint session on a mixed epic reaches the manual pause after autonomous work, handles each input variant correctly, and resumes."
- When this story is complete, killing sprint mid-pause (Ctrl-C, SIGURG, session kill) leaves ticket state recoverable: no manual story is transitioned to a terminal state without user input, no autonomous story is left in an indeterminate in_progress state
  Satisfies: "handles each input variant correctly, and resumes"
- When this story is complete, re-invoking sprint with an existing pause state file offers --resume; on --resume, the handshake rehydrates from the state file and continues from the last un-answered story
  Satisfies: "handles each input variant correctly, and resumes"
- When this story is complete, stale state files (older than TTL, default 4h mirroring merge-to-main) are cleaned up at sprint start with a diagnostic
  Satisfies: "handles each input variant correctly, and resumes"
- When this story is complete, with the flag off the pause state machinery never activates (no files written, no --resume path reached)
  Satisfies: "The entire feature sits behind a config flag ... When off, all four skills behave identically to current baseline."
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for state write/read, SIGURG-simulation recovery, stale-state cleanup, and --resume rehydration

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Pattern matches merge-to-main /tmp/merge-to-main-state-<branch>.json + --resume — align lifecycle and TTL to reduce operator cognitive load
- [Testing] SIGURG simulation requires a test harness that sends the signal mid-handshake; use the existing harness from INC-016 if applicable
- [Security] State file in /tmp is readable by other processes on the host — do not persist confirmation tokens in plaintext; persist a hash and re-derive on --resume if integrity check is needed

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
